data.title: LKQ authorizes $1B increase to stock buyback program
data.description:
• LKQ (NASDAQ:LKQ) said its board has authorized a $1 billion increase and a one-year extension to its stock repurchase program.
  • The aggregate authorization under the program has been raised to $4.5 billion and authorizing repurchases through October 25, 2026.

data.title: Las Vegas Sands gains as investors eye capital allocation moves
data.description:
Las Vegas Sands Corp. (NYSE:LVS) rose 1.7% in premarket trading on Thursday, despite the casino giant falling short of consensus estimates with its Q3 earnings report. Investors may have had their eyes on the capital allocation front, where the Las Vegas Sands (LVS) board of directors authorized $2.0 billion in future stock repurchases and announced a $0.20 increase in the recurring common stock dividend for the 2025 calendar year.

Jefferies analyst David Katz said lower-than-expected hold at Marina Bay Sands, disruptions created by renovations at The Londoner, as well as macroeconomic weakness in Macau highlighted the company's lower-than-expected Q3 performance. "We anticipate that as macro conditions improve and renovation-related disruptions decrease, the company could be better-positioned within Macau and Singapore," he added.

During the earnings call (transcript), CEO Robert Goldstein said the company believes that the Macau market gross gaming revenues will exceed $30 billion in 2025 and grow from there. He also pointed to considerable runway for growth in Singapore after LVS completes its investment programs in the region.

Shares of Las Vegas Sands (LVS) were up 1.67% in premarket trading to $51.89 vs. the 52-week range of $36.62 to $55.66. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on LVS is flashing Buy.

data.title: UPS rallies after solid earnings into the holiday season; FedEx also higher
data.description:
United Parcel Service (NYSE:UPS) rallied in early action on Thursday after topping consensus estimates with its third quarter earnings report. Revenue was up 5.4% year-over-year to $22.2 billion. "After a challenging 18-month period, our company returned to revenue and profit growth," stated CEO Carol Tomé. "Peak season is nearly upon us, and we are ready to deliver another successful holiday season and continue the progress we demonstrated in the third quarter," she added.

Revenue was up 5.8% in the U.S. Domestic segment to $14.45 billion, driven by a 6.5% increase in average daily volume. Revenue increased 3.4% in the International segment to $4.41 billion, driven primarily by a 2.5% increase in revenue per piece. Meanwhile, revenue rose 8.0% in the Supply Chains Solutions segment to $3.38 billion, due primarily to growth in air and ocean forwarding and the continued onboarding of USPS air cargo.

Consolidated operating profit was up 47.8% to $2.0 billion. UPS' (UPS) adjusted operating margin rate of 8.9% was ahead of the consensus estimate of 8.5%. Non-GAAP EPS was reported at $1.76 vs. $1.672 consensus and $1.57 a year ago. GAAP results included an after-tax net benefit of $36 million, or $0.04 per diluted share, comprised of a $152 million gain resulting from the divestiture of the Coyote Logistics business net of transformation strategy costs of $116 million.

Looking ahead, UPS (UPS) expects full-year consolidated revenue to be approximately $91.1 billion, vs. $91.9 billion consensus. UPS (UPS) said it sees dividend payments of around $4 billion for the year, subject to board approval.

Shares of UPS UPS) were up 5.01% in premarket action, while rival FedEx (FDX) gained 1.47%.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Valero Energy tops Q3 estimates, total throughput volumes drop y/y
Donegal Group reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Donegal Group press release (NASDAQ:DGICA): Q3 Net income of $16.8 million, or 51 cents per diluted Class A share, compared to net loss of $0.8 million, or 2 cents per Class A share
  • Net premiums earned increased 6.0% to $238.0 million
  • Net premiums written1 increased 5.9% to $232.2 million
  • Combined ratio of 96.4%, compared to 104.5%
  • Net income included after-tax net investment gains of $1.5 million, or 5 cents per diluted Class A share, compared to after-tax net investment losses of $1.0 million, or 3 cents per Class A share
  • Book value per share of $15.22 at September 30, 2024, compared to $14.26
